description: Trend Hunter CLI: sync the global trend feed, search the corpus offline, and pull rich trend metadata (FAQ Q&A, related trends, author, category) from the terminal. Free public-site scrape, no API key.

tools.name: category_list
tools.description: Fetch a TrendHunter category index page (~30 trend cards). The CLI's 'category <name>' command parses this. Required: category. Returns the RawHTML.

params.name: category
params.description: Category slug: tech, fashion, food, eco, ai, marketing, design, lifestyle, autos, gadgets, etc.

tools.name: megatrends_list
tools.description: Fetch the megatrend index page. Returns the RawHTML.

tools.name: popular_get
tools.description: Fetch the popular-trends index page (~30 cards). Run 'popular' to get parsed JSON. Returns the RawHTML.

tools.name: reports_list
tools.description: Fetch the trend-reports landing page (titles, descriptions, paid PDF links). Returns the RawHTML.

tools.name: results_query
tools.description: Run a TrendHunter search and get the result-page HTML. The CLI's 'search <term>' parses this; pass --raw to get HTML. Required: search. Returns the RawHTML.

params.name: search
params.description: Search query, e.g. 'AI clone' or 'sustainable fashion'

tools.name: rss_latest
tools.description: Fetch the global RSS 2.0 feed (~30 newest trends). Use 'sync' to land these in the local store; this raw endpoint is for one-off inspection. Returns the RawXML.

tools.name: scoreboard_get
tools.description: Fetch the scoreboard page with top contributor handles and recent trends. Run 'scoreboard' to get parsed JSON. Returns the RawHTML.

tools.name: sitemap_get
tools.description: Fetch the sitemap.xml. The CLI's 'catalog refresh' uses this; users should use that instead. Returns the RawXML.

tools.name: trends_get
tools.description: Fetch the full trend page HTML for a slug. The CLI's 'trend show <slug>' parses this; users should use that instead of calling this directly. Required: slug. Returns the RawHTML.

params.name: slug
params.description: Trend slug from a /trends/<slug> URL or RSS link, e.g. ai-clone
